things.name: Château de Brest
things.description: Ancient maritime fortress at the mouth of the River Penfeld, one of France's oldest castles housing the National Marine Museum

things.name: Océanopolis
things.description: Europe's finest marine discovery park with three pavilions recreating polar, tropical and temperate ocean environments

things.name: Pont de Recouvrance
things.description: Europe's largest vertical-lift bridge spanning the River Penfeld, an engineering landmark of the postwar rebuilding era

things.name: Rue de Siam
things.description: Brest's celebrated commercial boulevard commemorating the visit of Siamese ambassadors in 1686, lined with shops and cafes

things.name: Musée des Beaux-Arts de Brest
things.description: Fine arts museum with collections spanning the 16th to 20th centuries, including strong Pont-Aven School and Symbolist holdings

things.name: Place de la Liberté
things.description: Central square of rebuilt Brest with the Town Hall, fountain and vibrant street life at the heart of the modern city

things.name: Conservatoire Botanique National de Brest
things.description: Internationally renowned botanical garden specialising in threatened island species with extensive glasshouses and wild gardens

things.name: Presqu'île de Crozon
things.description: Spectacular Atlantic peninsula of dramatic cliffs and wild beaches near Brest, among Brittany's most beautiful coastline

things.name: Tour Tanguy Museum
things.description: Medieval tower beside the Penfeld showing dioramas of old Brest before its wartime destruction in a charming historic setting

things.name: Les Ateliers des Capucins
things.description: Enormous repurposed naval workshop of 1863 now a dynamic cultural venue with a skatepark, market, cinema and exhibitions
